title: Soft Shopping

content.statement: Testing buyer interest before you are ready to sell gives you the clearest business plan for increasing value.
content.question: If a buyer looked at your business today, what would stop them from paying a premium?
content.equation: Exit Value = Buyer Confidence × Strategic Fit × Reduced Risk
content.mental_model: Mirror held up by the market, buyers show you what your business is really worth.
content.business_lens: Take feedback from potential buyers and turn their objections into the next year’s improvement plan.
content.life_lens: Ask experienced people what would make your work more valuable before you desperately need the answer.
sop: Ask what would prevent a higher valuation
Turn those objections into the annual operating plan
content.explainer: Soft Shopping means casually taking the business to market or to experienced buyers for feedback before you actually need to sell. You are not begging for a deal. You are gathering market intelligence. Their hesitation is useful because it tells you exactly what the market does not yet trust.
questions: If a buyer looked at your business today, what would stop them from paying a premium?
Who in your network has actually bought or sold a business and would tell you the truth about yours?
What objection have you heard more than once that you still haven't fixed?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Buyer Confidence 6, Strategic Fit 8, Reduced Risk 5. Score = 6 times 8 times 5 = 240 out of a possible 1,000. Higher means buyers move quickly and pay closer to asking price. Lower means every conversation stalls at the same objections.
content.closing_statement: Let the market price your blind spots.

caption:
Principle Nº 253: Soft Shopping

Testing buyer interest before you are ready to sell gives you the clearest business plan for increasing value. Soft Shopping means casually taking the business to market or to experienced buyers for feedback before you actually need to sell.

Ask yourself: If a buyer looked at your business today, what would stop them from paying a premium?
